name: Ba Xian
primary_sources: The Eight Immortals Depart and Travel to the East
The Eight Immortals Cross the Sea

visual_attributes.morphology: group of eight distinct human figures, each with unique attributes
distinctive_features: He Xiangu holding a lotus flower
Li Tieguai with an iron crutch and calabash bottle
Lan Caihe with a basket of flowers
Zhang Guolao with a bamboo drum
visual_attributes.texture: varied textures including silk robes and metallic accessories
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: often depicted together in a harmonious group

domain_specific_visual.TAOIST_SYMBOLS: each immortal associated with a specific vessel or symbol
domain_specific_visual.ISLANDS: depicted on islands in the Bohai Sea

domain_specific_functional.CULTURAL_HEROES: serve as folk heroes and saints

relationships.description: He Xiangu is one of the Eight Immortals, often depicted holding a lotus flower.
Cao Guojiu is one of the Eight Immortals, related to a Song dynasty emperor.
Li Tieguai is one of the Eight Immortals, known for his iron crutch and calabash bottle.
Lan Caihe is one of the Eight Immortals, patron of florists and gardeners.
Lü Dongbin is one of the Eight Immortals, considered the leader of the group.
Han Xiangzi is one of the Eight Immortals, known for playing the flute.
Zhang Guolao is one of the Eight Immortals, symbol of longevity.
Zhongli Quan is one of the Eight Immortals, associated with death and wealth.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: represent prosperity, longevity, and Taoist virtues
primary_sources: The Eight Immortals Depart and Travel to the East
The Eight Immortals Cross the Sea
